profile_backed_target.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_WORKER=ts2 RCH_WORKERS=ts2 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- eval/maxpool_256x256_f64 --noplot --sample-size 10 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 2
profile_backed_target.basis: Fresh focused profile showed the literal-backed maxpool variant paying the generic per-window Literal path while the dense vec variant already used the typed max/min kernel.

optimization.lever: Route literal-backed DType::F64 rank-2 max/min reduce_window through reduce_window_f64_values so it packs tagged literals once, then reuses the existing typed max/min pooling kernel.

alien_graveyard.primitive: dense SoA plus stencil/windowed reduction over contiguous typed storage
alien_graveyard.mapping: Pack a declared-F64 Literal tensor into a contiguous f64 slab once, then evaluate the pooling stencil over native f64 values instead of materializing and matching a tagged Literal for every window element.

optimization.guard: rank == 2, dtype == F64, reduce_op in {max,min}, and reduce_window_f64_values(tensor) succeeds; all other cases use the previous generic path.

after.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 RCH_WORKER=ts2 RCH_WORKERS=ts2 rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- eval/maxpool_256x256_f64 --noplot --sample-size 10 --warm-up-time 1 --measurement-time 2

isomorphism_proof.ordering: Unchanged: output positions are emitted in row-major order, and each output cell scans window rows then window columns in the same order as the generic Literal accumulator path.
isomorphism_proof.padding: Unchanged: SAME and VALID geometry and pad lows are still computed by the shared caller before dispatch; the typed kernel keeps the same padded-row/col skip conditions.
isomorphism_proof.floating_point: Unchanged for valid F64 literals: reduce_window_f64_values copies each f64 value, then the typed kernel uses the same -inf/+inf initial accumulator and the same jax_max_f64/jax_min_f64 combiner, preserving NaN propagation and signed-zero behavior.
isomorphism_proof.tie_breaking: Not applicable; max/min returns values only, with no index/tie metadata.
isomorphism_proof.rng: Not applicable; reduce_window is deterministic and consumes no RNG.
isomorphism_proof.fallbacks: Malformed declared-F64 tensors, non-F64 dtypes, non-rank-2 operands, and non-max/min reduce ops do not satisfy the guard and therefore retain the previous generic behavior and error surface.
isomorphism_proof.output_storage: The optimized literal-backed case now returns dense F64 storage, matching the already-shipped dense path while preserving dtype, shape, element order, and f64 output bits.

golden.description: Golden records the literal-ref baseline, same-worker after timing, one-lever source change, score, and isomorphism obligations for literal-backed F64 max/min reduce_window packing.

validation.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-lax dense_f64_reduce_window_max_min_matches_generic --lib -- --nocapture
